description_long: This object is a tall, slender utility pole with a square cross-section, constructed from what appears to be wood with a natural brown finish. The pole is vertically mounted on a flat, square base that is marked with a grid pattern, likely for alignment or installation reference. The surface of the pole is smooth with visible wood grain texture, and there are no visible attachments such as wires, crossarms, transformers, or streetlights. The base is dark with a grid of red and blue lines, suggesting it is used for positioning or measurement rather than as a permanent fixture. The overall design is minimalistic, with no identification tags or additional hardware present.
description: A tall, brown wooden utility pole with a smooth surface, mounted on a square, grid-marked base and lacking any visible attachments or hardware.
description_view_0: Front view of the utility pole showing its slender, vertical form and the square, grid-marked base.
description_view_1: Side view of the utility pole, similar to the front, highlighting the uniform width and the grid pattern on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view of the utility pole, again showing the straight, unadorned shaft and the square base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, displaying the same features as other views with no visible attachments or markings.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: No crossarms are present on the utility pole.
